customer_anxiety_profile: Greg is not anxious in the conventional sense — he is a sophisticated, research-oriented customer who wants to be treated as a peer, not a prospect. His primary concern is not being pitched below his level. He has already internalized the savings argument and wants the financing/incentive structure conversation. He will disengage if the closer leads with basics he's already dismissed.
unresolved_concerns: Cost-effectiveness condition: Greg stated explicitly he would move forward 'if it's cost effective' by summer — this condition was never addressed or tied to the appointment.

Financing structures and cost-offset programs: Greg raised this twice and received only a generic deferral. The closer should be prepared to address cost-sharing programs, low-interest loan options, and leasing structures specifically.

Non-standard bill situation: Greg recently moved in, has no utility bill, is mid-renovation, and expects energy usage to change significantly. The closer cannot run a standard bill-based evaluation without addressing this.

Co-decision-maker unidentified: Greg used 'we' throughout the call and said 'when we come back' when considering tomorrow's availability. There is likely a co-decision-maker who has not been identified or included in the appointment.
sensitive_topics_raised: Two-structure property: Greg identified a second structure with a large, south-facing roof as the primary solar candidate — the closer should know this is the focus, not the main residence.
Environmental motivation: Greg explicitly stated renewable living and carbon footprint reduction as primary motivations alongside cost savings — the closer should not lead with a savings-only pitch.
Solar-as-HVAC-replacement strategy: Greg has a specific rationale — solar instead of expensive HVAC repair — that the closer should understand and validate rather than redirect.
HERS score planned for one structure: Greg mentioned a HERS energy efficiency score is forthcoming for one of the structures, which may affect system sizing.
exact_promises_rep_made: Tentative appointment set for Wednesday, April 22 at 1:00 PM.
Ion will reach out the day before to confirm the appointment.
The session will be blocked for one hour so Greg does not feel rushed.
The specialist will cover solar programs, state and local incentives, and financing options.
closer_should_know: Greg is a self-directed, research-literate customer who proposed the site visit himself and self-closed into a conditional commitment. He will not respond well to a standard pitch sequence. Lead with the two-structure, south-facing roof detail he volunteered at 01:41 — it signals you read the notes — then move directly to the financing and incentive structure conversation he asked for twice. His condition ('if it's cost effective') is the only live barrier; the closer's job is to resolve it with real numbers, not to re-sell the concept of solar.
